_comment: Ground truth for the synthetic OCR validation corpus. Recorded from an observed conversion run of corpus.pdf and reviewed, not written blind: whether a given LaTeX construct becomes an image crop is a property of the extractor, so elements are only asserted against what conversion actually produces. Regenerate the observation with 'python tools/build_ocr_corpus.py --report'. Equations are randomly composed standard forms, not taken from any source document.

_expected_class_values.formula: a crop is produced and must be read as mathematics
_expected_class_values.figure: a crop is produced and must be read as a figure
_expected_class_values.table: a crop is produced and must be read as a table
_expected_class_values.skip: a crop is produced but carries no content and must never reach the model
_expected_class_values.no_crop: conversion produces no crop for this element at all

_table_note: Table CROPS are now covered: TBL-002/003 are RASTER images (built by tools/build_ocr_corpus.py with PyMuPDF and included via graphicx), because a raster table has no text layer and so survives as an image crop. A LaTeX tabular (TBL-001) and a TikZ node-matrix both get their text extracted and produce no crop -- which is exactly why real table crops are rare (they occur only when a publisher's table has no recoverable text layer). Direct CropRef unit tests in tests/test_image_ocr.py still cover table geometry independently.
_figure_note: FIG-002..006 are pgfplots/TikZ vector graphics (bar chart, normal density, line-on-grid, Venn, path diagram). FIG-003 fragments into TWO crops -- the density area and a thin axis strip extracted separately -- both of which are figure content; a pgfplots axis line commonly detaches like this.

CORPUSMARK-EQ-001.note: numbered display equation, weighted arithmetic mean

CORPUSMARK-EQ-002.note: partial-derivative fraction with an improper integral

CORPUSMARK-EQ-004.note: multi-line align environment; taller than one line

CORPUSMARK-EQ-007.note: quantifiers and implication; aspect ~30, shape-identical to a decorative bar, separable only by compression

CORPUSMARK-EQ-009.note: UNNUMBERED display limit; aspect inside the figure/equation ambiguous band

CORPUSMARK-EQ-010.note: mixed italic and upright text with units

CORPUSMARK-EQ-011.note: display equation containing the phrase 'for all'; words must survive

CORPUSMARK-EQ-012.note: ADVERSARIAL: wide equation preceded by a 'Table 3 reports ...' cross-reference; must not be mistaken for a table

CORPUSMARK-EQ-013.note: ADVERSARIAL: wide equation preceded by a 'Figure 4 shows ...' cross-reference; must stay a formula

CORPUSMARK-EQ-015.note: binomial-as-product; its crop (659x213) also absorbs the neighbouring EQ-016 nested radical, which produces no separate crop

CORPUSMARK-EQ-016.note: nested radical; clustered into EQ-015's crop rather than producing its own, so asserted no_crop (its content is scored under EQ-015)

CORPUSMARK-EQBLOCK-001.note: a block of several stacked short equations; a squarer bounding box than a single line, the shape geometry misreads as a figure

CORPUSMARK-EQBOX-001.note: display equation inside a shaded definition box; both the words ('Definition', 'with') and the fraction must be recovered

CORPUSMARK-FIG-001.note: vector figure of line segments and points with a 'Fig. 1' caption and a picture-text marker

CORPUSMARK-FIG-003.note: pgfplots normal density; produces TWO crops (density area + detached axis strip), both figure

CORPUSMARK-FIG-004.note: line plot on a full grid; the ruled grid is a table-look trap

CORPUSMARK-FIG-005.note: Venn diagram of two events in a sample space

CORPUSMARK-TBL-002.note: raster contingency table; the only reliable way to synthesise a table CROP

CORPUSMARK-TBL-003.note: raster computational table of fractions; a wall of fractions is easily misread as one equation

CORPUSMARK-NEG-003.note: solid square block standing in for a logo

CORPUSMARK-NEG-004.note: ADVERSARIAL: decorative separator band deep inside the equation aspect band, given away only by near-zero compression complexity

CORPUSMARK-DEC-002.note: vertical gradient bar; a graphic with no text that must never be sent as a formula. A DELIBERATE heuristic blind spot: a gradient has moderate PNG complexity (compression floor misses it), carries no caption or picture-text marker, and its aspect (0.15) is not a landmark -- so the deterministic classifier cannot catch it and mislabels it 'formula'. This is exactly the decoration the VLM classifier exists to catch; encoding it here documents the boundary of geometry+compression and guards it in both directions.

CORPUSMARK-DEC-001.note: a small empty vector box does not cluster into an image crop; documents that a size gate never even sees it

CORPUSMARK-INLINE-001.note: inline mathematics stays in the text layer, never pulled into an image

CORPUSMARK-NEG-001.note: a single thin full-width rule is not clustered into an image

CORPUSMARK-TBL-001.note: LaTeX tabular content survives as text and is converted to Markdown directly, so no crop exists
